### p3870 Long stator configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration when operating a long stator motor.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Activate long stator help functions | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | Suppress Gx_ZSW.14 | Inactive | Active | - |

**Notice:**
  
The following restrictions apply to this function:  
- it is not permissible to change over the drive data set.  
- the encoder/drive may not be parked using a PROFIBUS telegram.  
- a maximum of 4 drives may be connected to the Control Unit.  
- it is not permissible to commutate with the zero mark (p0404).

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
All of the help functions for long stator motors can be enabled/disabled using this
bit.  
For bit 01:  
When the bit is set, bit 14 (parking encoder active) is set to 0 in the encoder status
word GX_ZSW independent of whether the encoder is parked or not.

### p3900 Completion of quick commissioning

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Exits quick commissioning (p0010 = 1) with automatic calculation of all parameters
of all existing drive data sets that depend on the entries made during quick commissioning.  
p3900 = 1 initially includes a parameter reset (factory setting, the same as p0970
= 1) for all parameters of the drive object; however, without overwriting the entries
made during the quick commissioning.  
The interconnections of PROFIBUS PZD telegram selection (p0922) and the interconnections
via p0700, p1000 and p1500 are re-established and all of the dependent motor, open-loop
and control-loop control parameters are calculated (corresponding to p0340 = 1).  
p3900 = 2 includes the restoration of the interconnections of PROFIBUS PZD telegram
selection (p0922) and the interconnections via p0700, p1000 and p1500 and the calculations
corresponding to p0340 = 1.  
p3900 = 3 only includes the calculations associated with the motor, open-loop and
closed-loop control parameters corresponding to p0340 = 1.

**Value:**
  
0:
No quick parameterization  
1:
Quick parameterization after parameter reset  
2:
Quick parameterization (only) for BICO and motor parameters  
3:
Quick parameterization for motor parameters (only)

**Notice:**
  
After the value has been modified, no further parameter modifications can be made
and the status is shown in r3996. Modifications can be made again when r3996 = 0.

**Note:**
  
When the calculations have been completed, p3900 and p0010 are automatically reset
to a value of zero.  
When calculating motor, open-loop and closed-loop control parameters (such as for
p0340 = 1) parameters associated with a selected Siemens catalog motor are not overwritten.

### p3981 Acknowledge drive object faults

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |

### p3985 Master control mode selection

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Setpoints | **Version:**5230300 |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode to change over the master control / LOCAL mode.

**Value:**
  
0:
Change master control for STW1.0 = 0  
1:

**Danger:**
  
When changing the master control in operation, the drive can manifest undesirable
behavior - e.g. it can accelerate up to another setpoint.

### p3990[0...n] Sequentially resets drive parameters

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**All groups | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 300 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Parameter to internally calculate subsequent (follow-on) parameters after a factory
setting was carried out.  
The parameter is then reset back to 0.

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Start a parameter reset  
3:
Start download of volatile parameters from RAM  
5:
Starts a safety parameter reset  
6:
Start reset non-safety/safety parameters  
10:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 10  
11:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 11  
12:
Start loading parameters saved with p0971 = 12  
30:
Start loading delivery condition saved with p0971 = 30  
100:
Start a BICO interconnection reset  
300:
Only Siemens internal

**Dependency:**
  
This parameter is called when first commissioning the system and also for factory
settings if this has been initiated via p0970 or p3900.  
  
Refer to:
p0970, p3900

### r3996[0...1] Parameter write inhibit status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**All objects | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Displays whether writing to parameters is inhibited.  
r3996[0] = 0:  
Parameter write not inhibited.  
0 &lt; r3996[0] &lt; 100:  
Parameter write inhibited. The value shows how the calculations are progressing.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Progress calculations  
[
1]:
Cause

**Note:**

Only for internal Siemens troubleshooting.

### p4630[0...n] Absolute encoder linear measuring step factor

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 1 | 4294967295 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Sets the resolution of the absolute position for a linear absolute encoder as factor
from p0407.

**Note:**
  
The serial protocol of an absolute encoder provides the position with a certain resolution
(e.g. 100 nm).  
The resolution is calculated from p0407/p4630.

### p4642 Encoder fault test function

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 255 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Test function to initiate an encoder fault  
For p4642 = 1:  
When setting, an encoder fault is output once at the currently used motor encoder.
Depending on the configuration in p0491, the drive switches over to encoderless operation
This means that encoderless motor stopping can be tested.  
For p4642 = 2:  
When setting, an encoder fault is output once at the currently used motor encoder.
For braking, the drive still uses the commutation position of the encoder. This means
that a faster motor stopping with encoder can be tested (only functions with SMx modules,
otherwise the same response as for p4642 = 1).  
The following generally applies:  
After acknowledgment, the encoder fault that was issued is cleared and p4642 is set
= 0.

**Notice:**
  
It is not permissible that a data set is switched over between an encoder fault being
issued and its deletion.

### r4648.0 CO/BO: Encoder status word

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the encoder status word.  
For bit 00:  
The bit is a group message for all encoders of the axis, and is set for the following
alarms:  
A3x407: "Function limit reached"  
A3x415: "Signal level track A or B out of tolerance (alarm)"  
A3x419: "Track A or B out of tolerance"  
A3x442: "Battery voltage alarm threshold reached"  
x = encoder 1, 2, 3

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Encoder maintenance required | No | Yes | - |

**Notice:**
  
Maintenance work may have to be carried out on the encoder.

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
For test purposes, the bit can be set using p4642 = 3.  
The bit is reset using p4642 = 0.

### p4649[0...n] Encoder function reserve amplitude limit incremental signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 500 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.1

**Description:**
  
Amplitude threshold of the incremental signals for the function reserve.  
If the set amplitude threshold on the incremental signals is fallen below, then alarm
A3x407 "Encoder x; function limit reached " is output.

**Note:**
  
An amplitude threshold of 230 mV can be used as default value.

### p4652[0...2] XIST1_ERW reset mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 4750 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode to reset the actual value in XIST_ERW (CO: r4653).

**Value:**
  
0:
Inactive  
1:
Reset with zero mark  
2:
Reset with BICO  
3:
Reset with selected zero mark

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Encoder 1  
[
1]:
Encoder 2  
[
2]:
Encoder 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4653, r4654, p4655

**Note:**
  
The absolute value is only valid after passing the zero mark.  
For value = 1:  
The value in XIST1_ERW is reset when passing every zero mark.  
For value = 2:  
The value in XIST1_ERW is reset with a 0/1 edge via binector input p4655.  
If value = 3:  
The value in XIST1_ERW is reset after a 0/1 edge via binector input p4655 when passing
the next zero mark.

## SINAMICS Parameter VECTORDM 04654 - 06673

SINAMICS Parameter VECTORDM 04654 - 06673

### r4654.0...16 CO/BO: XIST1_ERW status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 4750 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output to reset XIST1_ERW.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Encoder 1 XIST1_ERW reset | Low | High | - |
| 08 | Encoder 2 XIST1_ERW reset | Low | High | - |
| 16 | Encoder 3 XIST1_ERW reset | Low | High | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p4652, r4653, p4655

**Note:**
  
The reset of XIST1_ERW is initiated via binector input p4655.  
Binector output r4654 is reset with a 0 signal from binector input p4655.

### p4660[0...2] Sensor Module filter bandwidth

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [kHz] | 20000.00 [kHz] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [kHz] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the filter bandwidth for Sensor Module SMx10 (resolver) and SMx20 (sin/cos).  
The value set on the Sensor Module is displayed in r4661.  
Currently, the Sensor Module hardware only supports the following values:  
- 0: The Sensor Module's default setting is used.  
- 50 kHz  
- 170 kHz  
- 500 kHz  
- Unlimited: Only the bandwidth of the operational amplifier is effective.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Encoder 1  
[
1]:
Encoder 2  
[
2]:
Encoder 3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4661

**Note:**
  
A value of zero is displayed if an encoder is not present.

### p4662[0...n] Encoder characteristic type

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the characteristic type.  
For non-linear sensors, the interrelationship between the signal voltage and the position
can be defined using a third degree polynomial.

**Value:**
  
0:
Characteristic inactive  
1:
Characteristic polynomial third degree

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p4663, p4664, p4665, p4666

**Note:**
  
For value = 1:  
A third degree polynomial is defined as follows:  
F(x) = K3 * x^3 + K2 * x^2 + K1 * x + K0  
Coefficients K0 ... K3 should be defined and entered into p4663 ... p4666.  
The sensor range is emulated to x = -0.5 ... +0.5.

### p4670[0...n] Analog sensor configuration

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000 bin |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the configuration for evaluation on the analog sensor.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 06 | Set velocity to 0 | No | Yes | - |
| 08 | Position value range | -0.5 / +0.5 pulse | 0.0 / 1.0 pulse | - |
| 09 | Fault/alarm messages | Fault | Alarm | - |
| 10 | Channel B act | No | Yes | - |
| 11 | Channel A act | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Commutation angle constant | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Suppress faults | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Two-channel evaluation | No | Yes | - |
| 31 | Extrapolation | OFF | ON | - |

**Notice:**
  
For bit 06:  
Setting the bit sets the velocity actual value (r0061) permanently to 0.  
For bit 13:  
Setting the bit sets the commutation angle permanently to the commutation angle offset
(p0431).

**Note:**
  
For bit 09:  
A setting of bit = 0 will trigger a fault for the relevant channel if the actual value
is invalid.  
A setting of bit = 1 will trigger an alarm for the relevant channel if the actual
value is invalid.  
Zu Bit 10, 11:  
If both channels are activated, the actual value is generated from the average value
of both channels. If a channel fails (actual value invalid), it is not included when
the average value is generated.  
For bit 14:  
The bit is only evaluated for encoder 1. Otherwise no effect.  
For bit 15:  
When the bit is set, both input signals are separately evaluated and transferred.  
Prerequisite:  
Function "Extended cyclic DRIVE-CLiQ telegram" is activated (p0454.4 = 1).

### p4671[0...n] Analog sensor input

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the input circuit for the analog sensor.

**Value:**
  
0:
Differential  
1:
Single-ended A, B  
2:
Single-ended A*, B*  
3:
Single-ended A, B sensitive

**Note:**
  
p4671 = 0:  
The two signals on a track are evaluated differentially.  
p4671 = 1:  
Only the non-inverted signal on a track is evaluated.  
p4671 = 2:  
Only the inverted signal on a track is evaluated.  
p4671 = 3:  
Only the non-inverted signal on a track (high resolution) is evaluated.

|  |  |  |

| -10.0000 [V] | 10.0000 [V] | [ 0 ] 0.0000 [V] |

### p4673[0...n] Analog sensor channel A voltage per encoder period

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -10.0000 [V] | 10.0000 [V] | [ 0 ] 6.0000 [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the output voltage range to be mapped for the connected analog sensor.  
The voltage range is determined by the following parameters:  
- p4672 (voltage at actual value 0)  
- p4673 (voltage per encoder period)

**Note:**
  
The minimum actual value which can be mapped is equal to p4672 - p4673/2.  
The maximum actual value which can be mapped is equal to p4672 + p4673/2.

### p4680[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance permissible

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1000 | [ 0 ] 4 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the permissible tolerance in encoder pulses for the zero mark distance in the
context of zero mark monitoring.  
Causes fault F3x100 to appear less frequently.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F31100

### p4681[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance window limit 1 positive

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1000 | [ 0 ] 2 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the positive tolerance window in encoder pulses for limit 1 for the zero mark
monitoring.  
The pulse number is corrected if the deviation is less than this limit. If it is higher
than this limit, fault F3x131 is triggered.  
If fault F3x131 is re-parameterized to an alarm (A) or no message (N), then the encoder
pulses that are not corrected are transferred to the accumulator (p4688). The accumulator
can be deactivated using p0437.7.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0437, p4688  
Refer to:
F31131

**Note:**
  
This monitoring is activated by setting p0437.2 = 1 (position actual value correction).  
The positive limit describes additional pulses due to EMC.

### p4682[0...n] Zero mark monitoring tolerance window limit 1 negative

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 4) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer32 | **Dynamic index:**EDS, p0140 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, ENC | **P-Group:**Encoder | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -1001 | 0 | [ 0 ] -1001 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the negative tolerance window in encoder pulses for limit 1 for the zero mark
monitoring.  
The pulse number is corrected if the deviation is less than this limit. If it is higher
than this limit, fault F3x131 is triggered.  
If fault F3x131 is re-parameterized to an alarm (A) or no message (N), then the encoder
pulses that are not corrected are transferred to the accumulator (p4688). The accumulator
can be deactivated using p0437.7.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0437, p4681, p4688  
Refer to:
F31131

**Note:**
  
This monitoring is activated by setting p0437.2 = 1 (position actual value correction).  
For a set value = -1001, the negated value of p4681 becomes active.  
The negative limit describes the pulses lost due to a covered glass panel in the incremental
encoder.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

**Description:**
  
Sets the positive tolerance window in encoder pulses for limit 2 for the zero mark
monitoring.  
Accumulator (p4688) is compared with this parameter, and where relevant, alarm A3x422
is output for 5 seconds.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0437, p4681, p4682, p4688  
Refer to:
F31131, A31422

**Note:**
  
Zero mark monitoring is activated by setting p0437.2 = 1 (position actual value correction).

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

### p4862[0...15] Variable internal selection

|  |  |  |

information plus 1 character, minor information.

TEC: Technology Extension

TEC: Technology Extension

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

4.4

r4956[0]: Activation of Technology Extension 1  
r4956[1]: Activation of Technology Extension 2, ...

**Value:**
  
0:
Technology Extension inactive  
1:
Technology Extension active

Refer to:
r4950, r4951, r4952, r4955, r4957, r4958, r4959, r4960

**Notice:**
  
This parameter is only indexed if at least one drive object-specific Technology Extension
exists (p4950 &gt; 0).

**Note:**
  
TEC: Technology Extension

r4959[17]: Minor information of Technology Extension 1  
r4959[18...33]: GUID of Technology Extension 2  
r4959[34]: Major information of Technology Extension 2  
r4959[35]: Minor information of Technology Extension 2, ...

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4950, r4951, r4952, r4955, p4956, r4957, r4958, r4960

**Notice:**
  
This parameter is only indexed if at least one drive object-specific Technology Extension
exists (p4950 &gt; 0).

**Note:**
  
TEC: Technology Extension

### r4960[0...n] TEC DO-specific GUID drive object

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |

4.4

Displays the GUIDs of this drive object of the Technology Extensions installed on
the memory card/device memory.  
r4960[0...15]: GUID of this drive object of Technology Extension 1

r4960[17]: Minor information of this drive object of Technology Extension 1  
r4960[18...33]: GUID of this drive object of Technology Extension 2  
r4960[34]: Major information of this drive object of Technology Extension 2  
r4960[35]: Minor information of this drive object of Technology Extension 2, ...

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r4950, r4951, r4952, r4955, p4956, r4957, r4958, r4959

**Notice:**
  
This parameter is only indexed if at least one drive object-specific Technology Extension
exists (p4950 &gt; 0).

**Note:**
  
TEC: Technology Extension

### p5957[0...n] Build-up excitation completed voltage threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** 2661 |
| **Object:**VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 10.0 [%] | 150.0 [%] | [ 0 ] 95.0 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the voltage threshold of the separately excited synchronous motor.  
Above this threshold, the SESM is considered to have been excited.  
The voltage threshold refers to the setpoint of the DC link voltage (p3510).

**Notice:**
  
The factory setting should only be changed in exceptional cases.

**Note:**
  
SESM: separately excited synchronous motor

### r6221.2...24 CO/BO: Sequence control control command

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 2516 |
| **Object:**VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |

| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the control commands of the sequence control.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 02 | Power circuit switch on excitation | OFF | ON | - |
| 08 | Close incoming circuit breaker | OFF | ON | - |
| 10 | Switch on input transformer premagnetization | OFF | ON | - |
| 24 | Enable DC link monitoring | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6650

### p6277[0...n] Reverse field exciter speed setpoint rotating field inversion

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Setpoints | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -20000.00 [rpm] | 20000.00 [rpm] | [ 0 ] 0.00 [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the speed setpoint for rotating field inversion of the stator current in the
reverse field exciter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6278

### p6278[0...n] Reverse field excitation speed setp. rot. field inv. hysteresis

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**DDS, p0180 | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Setpoints | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**3_1 | **Unit selection:**p0505 |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2000 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| -20000.00 [rpm] | 20000.00 [rpm] | [ 0 ] 10.00 [rpm] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the hysteresis of the speed setpoint for rotating field inversion of the stator
current in the reverse field exciter.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6277

**Note:**
  
The amount of the value entered in the parameter is dynamically limited to the rated
speed of the motor.  
The value 0 is not permissible.  
The sign of the hysteresis defines the rotating field of the stator current for the
reverse field exciter depending on the mechanical direction of rotation.  
The hysteresis is symmetrical around the value in parameter p6277.

### r6279.13...15 CO/BO: Flux monitoring and speed monitoring state

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Motor | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the flux monitoring and speed monitoring.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 13 | Mech. direction of rotation from reverse field excitation | Turns anti-clockw. | Turns clockwise | - |
| 14 | Torque-generating abs current setpoint value less than threshold | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Torque-generating Iset * f is positive | Negative | Positive | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0063

**Note:**
  
For bit 00:  
The threshold value is set in p6270.  
For bit 01:  
The threshold value is set in p6272.  
For bit 03:  
The threshold value is set in p1226.  
For bit 04:  
The threshold value is set in p1752.  
For bit 05:  
The threshold value is set in p1404.  
For bit 07, 14:  
The threshold value is set in p6276.  
For bit 08:  
The threshold value is set in p6280.  
For bit 09:  
The threshold value is set in p6281.  
For bit 10:  
The threshold value is set in p6282.  
For bit 11:  
The threshold value is set in p6283.  
For bit 13:  
The threshold value is set in p6278.

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |

| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |

Displays the GUID of the hardware description data on the CompactFlash Card.  
The individual characters of the GUID are displayed as ASCII code in the form {XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX}
in the indices.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
F01275

**Note:**
  
An ASCII table (excerpt) can be found, for example, in the appendix to the List Manual.  
GUID: Globally Unique IDentifier

### r6547[0...10] HW description data A5E CF

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN, CU_MV_DP, VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Description:**
  
Displays the A5E number (CADIM) of the hardware description data on the CompactFlash
card.  
The individual characters of the CADIM number are displayed in the form A5EXXXXXXXX
as ASCII code.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6543, r6545  
Refer to:
F01275

**Note:**
  
An ASCII table (excerpt) can be found, for example, in the appendix to the List Manual.

on the NVRAM of the Control Unit. They can be read and changed using parameters p17750,
p17751 and p17752.

### r6625[0...4] DC link voltages status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**2 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [V] | - [V] | [ ] - [V] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays the status of the DC link voltages of the power unit.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
DC link voltage  
[
1]:
Permissible to reduce the DC link voltage  
[
2]:
DC link voltage from the supply voltage (p0210)  
[
3]:
DC link voltage reference  
[
4]:
DC link voltage from the actual supply voltage

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6639

**Note:**
  
For r6625[0]:  
Displays the DC link voltage corresponding with the voltage class of the power unit
(peak rectification).  
For r6625[1]:  
Displays the permissible DC link voltage, after taking into account the installation
altitude.  
For r6625[2]:  
Displays the DC link voltage resulting from the supply voltage (p0210).  
For r6625[3]:  
Display of the reference value of the DC link voltage is the same as r6625[2] for
a diode infeed or r6625[2] * step-up factor when the infeed is active.  
For r6625[4]:  
Displays the estimated (diode infeed) or measured (active infeed) from the DC link
voltage resulting from the actual supply voltage for peak rectification.  
Example:  
In order to explain the parameter indices and their interdependencies, the commissioning
of a fictitious power unit is described as an example. This involves a qualitative
example whose quantitative values are not generally valid.  
Assumption:  
Power unit with voltage class 3300 V, installation altitude 1000 m above sea level,
transformer tap at 3000 V, line supply voltage decreases, over the long term, by 5
%.  
Voltage class 3300 V:  
r6625[0] = sqrt(2) * 3300 V = 4667 V  
Before the installation altitude can be successfully entered into p6649, the line
supply voltage must be set to a lower value using p0210 (e.g. 2000 V).  
After p6649 = 1000 m is entered, the DC link voltage, resulting from the installation
altitude derating is taken into account.  
r6625[1] = sqrt(2) * 3300 V * 0.95 = 4433 V  
A transformer tap is now selected and therefore the supply voltage, whose peak rectification
value lies below r6625[1] (e.g. p0210 = 2800 V). This has the following result:  
r6625[2] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V = 3960 V  
If a drive with diode infeed is being used, the reference value of the DC link voltage
is also assigned this value; for a drive with active infeed, this is weighted with
the step-up factor.  
r6625[3] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V = 3960 V (diode infeed)  
r6625[3] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V * 1.08 = 4277 V (active infeed)  
Now, in operation, over the long term, the line supply voltage decreases by 5%:  
r6625[4] = sqrt(2) * 2800 V * 0.95 = 3762 V

### p6631 DC link voltage maximum value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8765 |
| **Object:**VECTORDM, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 70.000 [%] | 125.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 110.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the trip threshold for a fault due to an overvoltage in the DC link.  
The threshold refers to the maximum permissible DC link voltage that is obtained taking
into consideration the installation altitude, depending on the device description
data (r6625[1]).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6625, p6627

### p6633 DC link voltage minimum value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8765 |
| **Object:**VECTORDM, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 50.000 [%] | 85.000 [%] | [ 0 ] 75.000 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the trip threshold for a fault due to an undervoltage in the DC link.  
The threshold refers to the maximum permissible DC link voltage that is obtained taking
into consideration the installation altitude, depending on the device description
data (r6625[1]).

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r6625, p6627

**Notice:**
  
For GH150, p6633 may be reduced to 50%. This means that operation can be maintained
even for undervoltage conditions if parameter p7532 (minimum DC link voltage on VECTORM2C)
is appropriately reduced.  
For all other products, only a value down to a minimum of 70% is permitted.

**Note:**
  
PSA: Power Stack Adapter

### p6641 CI: Phase failure monitoring DC link current signal source

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORDM, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2002 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source for the DC link current actual value complete component.  
This actual value is used to monitor power pulsations (F30029).  
The following BICO interconnection should be set:  
CI: p6641 (infeed) = CO: r0086[0] (drive)

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r0086, p0514, p0522, p6642  
Refer to:
F30029

### p6642 Phase failure monitoring threshold value

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORDM, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.00 [%] | 100.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 5.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Sets the threshold value for the phase failure monitoring function.  
For phase failure, pulsation occurs in the DC link with twice the line frequency.  
The percentage value is referred to the rated power (hardware description data).  
Fault F30029 is output when the set threshold value is exceeded.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6641  
Refer to:
F30029

**Note:**
  
Phase failure monitoring is deactivated for p6642 = 0.00 %.

| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORDM | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 34 | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Setting to activate/deactivate test mode for sequence control.  
For test mode [80]: the CU and DSM should be switched off and on again after the update.

**Value:**
  
0:
Test operation inactive  
33:
Test operation excitation  
34:
Test operation excitation with setpoint from precontrol

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p0840, p6651  
Refer to:
A07318

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the test mode.

**Caution:**
  
Bit r0899.3 (fault present) can be set with delay in the status word due to the message
generation.

**Note:**
  
The test is started when the logical AND operation of the signals of binector inputs
p6651 and p0840 is fulfilled.

### p6655 Acknowledgment external pulse time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Commands | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.0 [s] | 5.0 [s] | [ 0 ] 1.0 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.5

**Description:**
  
Sets the pulse time for the external acknowledgment.

**Note:**
  
The total duration of the acknowledgment procedure is extended by this time.  
The pulse time has been selected too short if a fault from the external peripherals
must be acknowledged twice.

### r6660.0...5 CO/BO: Switch 0 control signals

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned8 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Displays (binector outputs) the control signals for switch 0.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | "Close" command | Inactive | Active | - |
| 01 | "Open" command | Active | Inactive | - |
| 02 | Command "open undervoltage coil" | Active | Inactive | - |
| 03 | Command "open higher-level switch" | Inactive | Active | - |
| 04 | Software feedback signal closed | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Software feedback signal open | No | Yes | - |

**Note:**
  
For bit 03:  
This signal is set if, as a result of the command "open switch", no feedback signal
was detected and the crowbar thyristor was fired. It can be used to switch off the
plant/system by means of a higher-level switch.

### p6670[0...5] BI: Switch simulation mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source of the simulation mode for the switch.  
When the simulation mode is activated, the feedback signals for the switch are set
the same as the software commands. All monitoring functions are deactivated.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Warning:**
  
Only appropriately trained service and maintenance personnel may select and execute
the simulation mode.

**Note:**
  
BI: p6670[x] = 1 signal: Simulation mode activated  
BI: p6670[x] = 0 signal: Simulation mode deactivated

### p6671[0...5] BI: Switch on/off

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal source to close/open the switch.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Note:**
  
The switch 0 (main switch) is only opened/closed via the sequence control, i.e. binector
input p6671[0] is ineffective.  
BI: p6671[1...4] is used to control optional switches.

## SINAMICS Parameter VECTORDM 06674 - 08790

SINAMICS Parameter VECTORDM 06674 - 08790

### p6674[0...5] Switch close monitoring time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**1 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.000 [s] | 3600.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.150 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time for the feedback signal when closing the switch.  
After a CLOSE command, the feedback signal must be received within this time.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6675  
Refer to:
F07312

### p6675[0...5] Switch open monitoring time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.160 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the monitoring time of the feedback signals when opening the switch.  
The feedback signal within the time in p6675 must indicate that the switch has reliably
opened (i.e. bounce can no longer be detected). The monitoring time in p6675 must
be set greater than the time in p6676 as it additionally takes into account the bounce
time of the feedback signal.  
The following applies: p6675 &gt; p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6674, p6676  
Refer to:
F07311

**Danger:**
  
If this monitoring function responds, then it must be assumed that the switch had
not opened. In this case, the drive system is not disconnected from the line supply.

### p6676[0...5] Switch max. opening time

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C2( 1, 2) | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C | **P-Group:**Converter | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0.030 [s] | 20.000 [s] | [ 0 ] 0.100 [s] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the maximum permissible opening time of the switch.  
The feedback signal must indicate for the first time that the switch has opened within
the time in p6676. The feedback signal must be stable within the time in p6675 (i.e.
bounce can no longer be detected).  
The following applies: p6675 &gt; p6676

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Switch 0  
[
1]:
Switch 1  
[
2]:
Switch 2  
[
3]:
Switch 3  
[
4]:
Switch 4  
[
5]:
Switch 5

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6675  
Refer to:
F07306

**Danger:**
  
The switch used must open within this time in order to protect the drive system.  
Switch with a longer opening time than specified in this parameter cannot be used.

| 08 | External trip | No | Yes | - |

### r6727 CO: Offset commutation angle to pole wheel angle

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 4715 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**p2005 | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - [°] | - [°] | [ ] - [°] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Offset angle between the electrical commutation angle (from the encoder) and the electrical
pole wheel angle.

**Note:**
  
If there is still no offset, cannot be determined – or if, with an encoder fault active,
value 3600 is displayed.  
The offset angle itself is either determined during the pole wheel position identification
(= rotor position sensing) with an incremental encoder, or from the absolute position
of the absolute encoder.  
The offset is also determined after an encoder fault has been resolved from the zero
track of the incremental encoder or from the absolute position.  
However, the precondition is that the setting is not equal to the factory setting
of parameter p6729.

### p6814 Braking chopper DC link voltage threshold

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**FloatingPoint32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 7622 |
| **Object:**VECTORDM, B_INFMV | **P-Group:**Closed-loop control | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**PERCENT | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 30.00 [%] | 135.00 [%] | [ 0 ] 120.00 [%] |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the overvoltage threshold in the DC link.  
The value is referred to r6625[1].  
If the actual value of the DC link voltage r0070[0] exceeds this threshold, then a
negative current setpoint is generated for the braking chopper and is made available
at connector output r6818 for interconnection; for example, to switch on the braking
chopper.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6817, r6818

current setpoint for the braking chopper.

**Recommend.:**
  
Interconnect connector inputs p6849[0...3] (DO: BMM2C) with r6818 of all existing
drive objects.

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6817

### r6992.0...15 CO/BO: Recorder status word

| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and BICO output for the status word of the recorder.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Internal activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 01 | External activation | Not set | Set | - |
| 02 | Internal trigger | Not set | Set | - |
| 03 | External trigger 1.1 | Not set | Set | - |
| 04 | External trigger 1.2 | Not set | Set | - |
| 05 | External trigger 1.3 | Not set | Set | - |
| 06 | External trigger 1.4 | Not set | Set | - |
| 07 | External trigger 2.1 | Not set | Set | - |
| 08 | External trigger 2.2 | Not set | Set | - |
| 09 | External trigger 2.3 | Not set | Set | - |
| 10 | Hardware trigger | Not set | Set | - |
| 11 | Data buffering running | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Post trigger time running | No | Yes | - |
| 13 | Data being stored | No | Yes | - |
| 14 | Data buffer full | No | Yes | - |
| 15 | Trigger group signal | Not set | Set | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6993, p6994, r6997, p6998, p6999  
Refer to:
A49998

### p6993[0...5] Recorder trigger 2 bit mask

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | FFFF FFFF hex | [ 0 ] 0001 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Sets the bit mask for trigger signal 2 (p6994) of the recorder.  
External trigger 2.1 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[0] and the bit mask in p6993[0].  
External trigger 2.2 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[1] and the bit mask in p6993[1].  
External trigger 2.3 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[2] and the bit mask in p6993[2].  
External trigger 2.4 is formed from the rising edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[3] and the bit mask in p6993[3].  
External trigger 2.5 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[4] and the bit mask in p6993[4].  
External trigger 2.6 is formed from the falling edge of ANDing the signal source in
p6994[5] and the bit mask in p6993[5].

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External trigger 2.1  
[
1]:
External trigger 2.2  
[
2]:
External trigger 2.3  
[
3]:
External trigger 2.4  
[
4]:
External trigger 2.5  
[
5]:
External trigger 2.6

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p6994

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.

### p6998[0...4] BI: Recorder trigger 1 signal sources

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 / Binary | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ 0 ] 0 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the signal sources to activate and trigger the recorder.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
External activation  
[
1]:
External trigger 1.1  
[
2]:
External trigger 1.2  
[
3]:
External trigger 1.3  
[
4]:
External trigger 1.4

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.  
For index [0]:  
The external recorder activation extends the internal activation. If the recorder
has already been activated internally, then it can be externally triggered via p6998[1..4]
or p6994[0..2], without requiring that p6998[0] = 1.

### p6999[0...8] Recorder parameterization

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 8144, 8145 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 2000 | [ 0 ] 1   [ 1 ] 2000   [ 2 ] 1900   [ 3 ] 0   [ 4 ] 0   [ 5 ] 100   [ 6 ] 1   [ 7 ] 1   [ 8 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.8

**Description:**
  
Setting to parameterize the recorder.  
The recorder supplies up to 256 internal variables (depending on the parameterization).
The maximum recording time is 2000 ms. The variables are acquired in an integer multiple
of the current controller sampling time – and a pretrigger can be set. The values
can then be acyclically written to the memory card.  
The development and system test departments have the software necessary to decode
the content.  
Normally, this recorder is always enabled. However, if problems occur for topologies
with a low remaining CPU time, then it can be deactivated using this parameter (this
saves approx. 5 ... 20 µs CPU time).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
Enable  
[
1]:
Recording time  
[
2]:
Pretrigger time internal trigger  
[
3]:
Output message  
[
4]:
Recording factor  
[
5]:
Pretrigger time external trigger  
[
6]:
Hardware trace in synchronism  
[
7]:
Hardware trigger active  
[
8]:
Recording sampling time factor

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
A49998

**Note:**
  
If default settings are defined in the DESC0020 file associated with the drive object,
then after a POWER ON, the indices of the parameter are set to these default settings.
This means that any parameter assignments that were made could be overwritten.  
For index [0]:  
Enables or disables the function.  
p6999[0] = 0  
Inhibits the function.  
p6999[0] = 1  
Enables the function. A POWER ON is required after the enable.  
p6999[0] = 20  
Sets the factory setting for p6999, p6996, p6993, p6994, p6998.  
For index [1]:  
Sets the recording time, [ms]. The value is discretized with the current controller
sampling time.  
For index [2]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the internal trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [3]:  
Enables or inhibits the output of message A49998 when a trigger event is initiated.  
For index [4]:  
p6999[4] = n, n = 0 ... 4  
Recording with the factor, which extends the record time p6999[1] and the pre-trigger
time p6999[2] 2^n times, and reduces the number of signals 2^n times.  
Example:  
p6999[1] = 2000, p6999[2] = 1000, p6999[4] = 4  
--&gt; recording time: 2000 ms * 2^4 = 32 s, pre-trigger time:1000 ms * 2^4 = 16 s, number
of signals 128/16 = 8  
When setting p6999[4] to 0, the results are as follows:  
--&gt; recording time: 2000 ms, pre-trigger time: 1000 ms, number of signals: 128  
The maximum number of signals depends on the drive object.  
For index [5]:  
Sets the pretrigger time [ms] of the external trigger. This time is included in the
recording time and cannot be longer than the recording time p6999[1]. The value is
discretized with the current controller sampling time.  
For index [6]:  
Activating/deactivating wait time for a hardware trace to be completely uploaded.  
p6999[6] = 1  
After a trigger event, the system waits for the hardware trace upload to be completed.
A new trigger is not possible until this upload has been completed.  
p6999[6] = 0  
After a trigger event, the system does NOT wait for the hardware trace upload to be
completed. Recorder trace can be immediately re-triggered.  
For index [7]:  
Deactivates/activates the hardware trigger. When p6999[7] = 0, the hardware trigger
is deactivated, and a hardware fault means that the recorder is no longer triggered.  
For index [8]:  
Sets the factor for the recording sampling time.  
Example:  
p6999[8] = 2 --&gt; sampling time to record/trace signals is 2 x current controller sampling
time  
After a power on, this parameter has the same value as before the change.

### r7100[0...99] Par_circuit ring buffer fault/alarm code

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**Displays, signals | **Version:**5230300 |

| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Ring buffer for faults and alarms that have occurred from power units connected in
parallel (Motor Module, Active Line Infeed, Voltage Sensing Module).  
Displays the fault/alarm code.

The parameter is reset to 0 at POWER ON.

### r7123[0...99] Par_circuit ring buffer fault/alarm received in days

|  |  |  |

| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, BMM2C | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
5.2

**Description:**
  
Displays the relative system runtime in days when the fault/alarm occurred.  
Ring buffer for faults and alarms that have occurred for power units connected in
parallel.

**Notice:**
  
The time comprises r7123 (days) and r7102 (milliseconds).  
The time display depends on the selected mode (p3100).

**Note:**
  
The last fault case that occurred is documented in index 0.  
The parameter is reset to 0 at POWER ON.

### r7760 Write protection/know-how protection status

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTORGL, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Displays the status for the write protection and know-how protection.

**Bit field:**

|  |  |  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bit | Signal name | 0 signal | 1 signal | Function diagram |
| 00 | Write protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 01 | Know-how protection active | No | Yes | - |
| 02 | Know-how protection temporarily withdrawn | No | Yes | - |
| 03 | Know-how protection cannot be deactivated | No | Yes | - |
| 04 | Extended copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 05 | Basic copy protection is active | No | Yes | - |
| 06 | Trace and measuring functions for diagnostic purposes active | No | Yes | - |
| 12 | Reserved, Siemens-internal | No | Yes | - |

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p7761, p7765

**Note:**
  
KHP: Know-How Protection  
For bit 00:  
Write protection can be activated/deactivated via p7761 on the Control Unit.  
For bit 01:  
The know-how protection can be activated by entering a password (p7766 ... p7768).  
For bit 02:  
If it has already been activated, know-how protection can be temporarily deactivated
by entering the valid password in p7766. In this case, bit 1 = 0 and bit 2 = 1 offset.  
For bit 03:  
Know-how protection cannot be deactivated, as p7766 is not entered in the OEM exception
list (only the factory setting is possible). This bit is only set if know-how protection
is active (bit 1 = 1) and p7766 has not been entered in the OEM exception list.  
For bit 04:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards/Control
Units. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and in p7765.0 is set
= 1.  
For bit 05:  
When know-how protection has been activated, the contents of the memory card (parameter
and DCC data) can be additionally protected against being used with other memory cards.
This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.1 is set = 1 and p7765.0
is set = 0.  
For bit 06:  
When know-how protection is activated, the drive data can be traced using the device
trace function. This bit is only set if know-how protection is active and p7765.2
is set = 1.  
For bit 12:  
Together with p7755, the bit is used to monitor write protection.  
Bit = 1, if p7755 is not equal to 0 and write protection is active (r7760.0 = 1).  
Bit = 0, if write protection was deactivated. p7755 is set to 0, and when write protection
is activated again, bit 12 remains at 0.

p7764[0...n], with n = p7763 - 1

to.

to.

### p7857 Sub-boot mode

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**UT | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**4 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV, VECTOR3P, VECTORSL, VECTORM2C, VECTORDM, A_INFMV, B_INFMV, A_INFM2C, TECBASE, TM31, TM15DI_DO, TM150, TB30, BMM2C, ENC, HUB | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 1 | [ 0 ] 1 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.4

**Description:**
  
Sets the mode for the sub-boot.

**Value:**
  
0:
Sub-boot manual  
1:
Sub-boot automatic

**Note:**
  
For p7857 = 0 (manual sub-boot) the following applies:  
The parameter should be set to 1 to start the sub-boot.

### p8612[0...1] CAN drive object server SDO

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**CU_MV_PN (CAN), CU_MV_DP (CAN), VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0581 hex | 8000 067F hex | [ 0 ] 8000 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the identifier (client/server and server/client) of the additional SDO server.  
Using these SDO servers, it is possible to access manufacturer-specific CANopen objects
of the supported drive objects.

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
COB-ID drive object from the client to the server  
[
1]:
COB-ID drive object from the server to the client

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
r8610

**Note:**
  
SDO: Service Data Object  
Regarding the drive object Control Unit:  
- Corresponds to the CANopen object 1201 hex  
Regarding the drive object with closed-loop control functions:  
- Corresponds to the CANopen object 1202 hex + 1 * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7)

### p8641 CAN Abort Connection Option Code

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0 | 3 | [ 0 ] 3 |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the fault response for a CAN communication error with output of fault F08700.  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 6007 hex.

**Value:**
  
0:
No response  
1:
OFF1  
2:
OFF2  
3:
OFF3

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p2100, p2101  
Refer to:
F08700

**Note:**
  
When the factory setting is changed, the fault response of fault F08700 is written
to p2100 and p2101. In this particular case it is not possible to change the message
type using p2118 and p2119.

### p8700[0...1] CAN Receive PDO 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9204, 9206 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | 8000 06DF hex | [ 0 ] 8000 06DF hex   [ 1 ] 00FE hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the communication parameters for CANopen Receive Process Data Object 1 (RPDO
1).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PDO COB-ID  
[
1]:
PDO transmission type

**Dependency:**
  
A valid COB-ID can only be set for the available (existing) channel.

**Note:**
  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 1400 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1, FE and FF can be set.  
PDO: Process Data Object

3]:

as invalid.

### p8720[0...4] CAN Transmit PDO 1

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:** C1( 3) T | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Unsigned32 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** 9208, 9210 |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**Communications | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**- | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| 0000 hex | C000 06DF hex | [ 0 ] C000 06DF hex   [ 1 ] 00FE hex   [ 2 ] 0000 hex   [ 3 ] 0000 hex   [ 4 ] 0000 hex |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Sets the communication parameters for CANopen Transmit Process Data Object 1 (TPDO
1).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PDO COB-ID  
[
1]:
PDO transmission type  
[
2]:
Inhibit time (in 100 µs)  
[
3]:
Reserved  
[
4]:
Event timer (in ms)

**Dependency:**
  
A valid COB-ID can only be set for the available (existing) channel.

**Notice:**
  
For inhibit time and event timer, the following apply:  
A value that is not a multiple integer of the CANopen sampling time is rounded-off.

**Note:**
  
Corresponds to the CANopen object 1800 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
Transmission types 0, 1 ... F0, FE and FF can be set.  
p8848: CANopen sampling time  
PDO: Process Data Object

Corresponds to the CANopen object 1A04 hex + 40 hex * x (x: Drive number 0 ... 7).  
The parameter can only be written online when the associated COB ID in p872x is set
as invalid.

### r8745[0...15] CO: CAN free PZD receive objects 16 bit

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Can be changed:**- | **Calculated:**- | **Access level:**3 |
| **Data type:**Integer16 | **Dynamic index:**- | **Func. diagram:** - |
| **Object:**VECTORMV (CAN), VECTORGL (CAN), VECTOR3P (CAN), VECTORSL (CAN), VECTORM2C (CAN), VECTORDM (CAN), A_INFMV (CAN), B_INFMV (CAN), A_INFM2C (CAN), BMM2C (CAN) | **P-Group:**- | **Version:**5230300 |
| **Units group:**- | **Unit selection:**- |  |
| **Not for motor type:**- | **Scaling:**4000H | **Expert list:**1 |
| **Min** | **Max** | **Factory setting** |
| - | - | [ ] - |
| [Alarms](SINAMICS%20Alarms%20VECTORDM.md#sinamics-alarms-vectordm) |  |  |

**Valid as of version:**
  
4.7

**Description:**
  
Display and connector output of the free PZD receive objects (16 bit) using SDO transfer.  
An index does not contain a sensible value if the corresponding object is mapped in
one of the 8 possible RPDO (p8710 ... p8717). In this case, the interconnection and
the SDO transfer must be realized via PZD interface 2 (r8850[x]).

**Index:**
  
[
0]:
PZD object 0  
[
1]:
PZD object 1  
[
2]:
PZD object 2  
[
3]:
PZD object 3  
[
4]:
PZD object 4

5]:
PZD object 5

[
12]:
PZD object 12  
[
13]:
PZD object 13  
[
14]:
PZD object 14  
[
15]:
PZD object 15

**Dependency:**
  
  
Refer to:
p8710, p8711, p8712, p8713, p8714, p8715, p8716, p8717, r8750, r8850

**Note:**
  
RPDO: Receive Process Data Object  
SDO: Service Data Object  
Index 0 corresponds to the CANopen object 5800 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 1 corresponds to the CANopen object 5801 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 2 corresponds to the CANopen object 5802 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).  
Index 3 corresponds to the CANopen object 5803 hex + 80 hex * x (x: Drive number 0
... 7).

... 7).

... 7).

... 7).

... 7).

... 7).

... 7).

0 ... 7).

7]:
